mappings.rationale: Directly covers Buddhism including its history, doctrines, schools (Theravada, Mahayana), practices, and institutions — the primary subject of section 824.
Covers the life, work, and teachings of Siddhartha Gautama, directly relevant to 824.A.2 on the founding of Buddhism.
Covers Buddhist ethical practices and doctrines, relevant to 824.C.3.a on universal or prevalent ethical and religious practices.
Directly covers Tibetan Buddhism, relevant to 824.A.6 on Buddhism in Tibet, 824.C.2.c on esoteric Buddhism and Tantrism.
Covers Indian philosophical traditions including Buddhist philosophy, relevant to 824.B.3 on the role of systematic reflective thought in Buddhism.
Provides context for Buddhism's Hindu cultural background, directly relevant to 824.A.1 on the cultural context and background in Hinduism.
Confucianism interacted with Buddhism in China, Korea, and Japan, relevant to 824.A.4 and 824.A.5 on Buddhism's development in East Asia.
Covers modern Japan where Buddhism remains a major religion, relevant to 824.A.5 on Buddhism in Japan and 824.A.8 on Buddhism in the modern era.
Covers modern China where Buddhism has a long history of development, relevant to 824.A.4 on Buddhism in China.
Korea has a significant Buddhist tradition, relevant to 824.A.5 on Buddhism in Korea.
Covers Southeast Asia where Theravada Buddhism is predominant, relevant to 824.A.7 on Buddhism in Ceylon and Southeast Asia.
Covers philosophical questions about the nature of reality and the divine relevant to Buddhist philosophical positions on impermanence and the absence of self in 824.C.1.a.
Covers mystical and meditative traditions relevant to understanding Buddhist mysticism and meditation described in 824.B.4.
Provides comparative context for understanding Buddhism within the broader study of religion.
Covers approaches to myth relevant to understanding Buddhist mythology including its basic types, contents, and functions described in 824.B.2.
The Silk Road was a major conduit for the spread of Buddhism from India to Central Asia and China, relevant to 824.A.4.
